item.title: Eurogliders founder Bernie Lynch dies age 72
item.description: Australian music icon Bernie Lynch wrote all of Eurogliders's biggest songs, including the smash hit Heaven (Must Be There).

item.title: Cliff collapse at Port Willunga prompts Adelaide council warning
item.description: A South Australian mayor says no-one was hurt when boulders fell from a cliff face at a popular beach in Adelaide's south.

item.title: Diesel thefts rise in regional NSW as prices soar amid Middle East war
item.description: The NSW Police Rural Crime Team is reporting a spike in diesel being stolen from landholders amid soaring prices.

item.title: Scientists say summer of weather whiplash points to driving force of climate change
item.description: Scientists warn sharp swings from extreme heat to flooding this summer signal climate change is overtaking other weather drivers.

item.title: Letters to hit-and-run victim's family 'vital' in Albany cold case investigation
item.description: WA detectives have travelled to the Great Southern city of Albany to re-investigate the hit-and-run death of Mark Edward Gregory in 1991.

item.title: RBA lifts interest rates by 0.25pc for second time this year
item.description: The Reserve Bank has lifted interest rates by 0.25 percentage points as the war in the Middle East sends oil, gas, and petrol prices soaring.

item.title: Izak Rankine to return for Crows after AFL homophobic slur ban, Swans back Charlie Curnow
item.description: Izak Rankine is set to play his first AFL match in 2026 since serving a four-match ban for a homophobic slur.

item.title: MAGA's civil war over Donald Trump's conflict in Iran descends into personal attacks
item.description: A civil war within the MAGA movement is getting more lewd by the day as prominent right-wing figures feud online over the war in Iran.

item.title: Cadia goldmine pollution allegations lead to class action against Newmont
item.description: A small farming community in central west NSW is taking on mining giant Newmont, alleging a goldmine at Cadia, near Orange, has contaminated their agricultural assets.

item.title: Identity of street artist Banksy revealed in new investigation
item.description: An in-depth investigation drawing on court files, photos and public records reports the world-famous anonymous artist is a Bristol-born man who later adopted a new identity.

item.title: Ipswich woman charged with murder of Katherine Sanowski
item.description: A 34-year-old woman has been charged over the alleged murder of Springfield woman Katherine Sanowski, who was found in an Ipswich home last month.

item.title: Mortgage repayment calculator: How much extra you could be paying now the cash rate is 4.1 per cent
item.description: Tell us your loan size, term and interest rate, and our mortgage repayment calculator will work out how much extra you may have to pay on your loan.

item.title: Manildra ordered to pay $170,000 after silos spill grain into Shoalhaven River
item.description: The Environment Protection Authority has ordered Manildra to spend $170,000 on community projects after its grain silos collapsed into the Shoalhaven River almost two years ago.

item.title: Economists call for budget bravery as rising inflation contributes to another RBA rate rise
item.description: Economist are urging the government make brave budget calls as Australian households prepare to sweat through another rate rise.

item.title: Federation Council to remove Indigenous flags despite overwhelming community support
item.description: Despite strong opposition, the council on the NSW-Victoria border will remove Indigenous flags from its chamber after it was revealed there were no protocols to display them.

item.title: What is vaginismus and how do you know if you have it?
item.description: Madeleine Edwards had never heard of the term vaginismus before she was diagnosed with the condition.

item.title: Aerial vision of Mooloolah River Interchange
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Navigation sounders turned off before Nuyina hit ocean floor, ATSB report notes
item.description: A preliminary report has revealed new details about how an Australian research vessel hit the ocean floor after navigational echo sounders were turned off to stop interference with scientific sampling.

item.title: Man who killed Perth lawyer Alice McShera at Crown Towers sentenced to life in prison
item.description: Cameron Pearson, who pleaded guilty to killing Perth barrister Alice McShera by bashing her in the head with a champagne bottle, will spend at least 19 years in jail.

item.title: Major banks announce interest rate increase after Reserve Bank lifts cash rate by 0.25 per cent
item.description: Australia's four major banks respond to the Reserve Bank of Australia's decision to increase the national cash rate to 4.1 per cent.

item.title: What are the best ways to save fuel?
item.description: From fuel-efficient driving to alternative travel modes, we look at practical ways to combat fuel shortages and price hikes.

item.title: Bunbury masseur Anthony Brain sentenced to more than 15 years jail for raping clients
item.description: A masseur who described himself as a nudist is jailed for 15-and-a-half years for sexually assaulting more than a dozen female clients.

item.title: Kyle Sandilands says he hopes ARN Media contract will be honoured
item.description: Radio broadcaster Kyle Sandilands says he is considering "many options" after being taken off-air for insulting his co-host and friend of almost 30 years Jackie "O" Henderson.

item.title: Man charged with murder of woman and baby in Belivah
item.description: A woman and a baby were found dead inside their Belivah home on Thursday morning when police attended the house to advise that the man had been hit by a car.

item.title: Childcare worker Arvind Ajay Singh on trial for alleged rape of child
item.description: A childcare worker is standing trial over allegations he raped a young child on Queensland's Sunshine Coast.

item.title: Defence minister confirms US can access data collected by Australian surveillance plane in Gulf
item.description: The defence minister has confirmed intelligence gathered by an Australian surveillance aircraft deployed to support Gulf states can be accessed by the United States, potentially informing its strikes on Iran.

item.title: Men accused of killing Canberra grandmother Irma Palasics 'went there for money, not murder', jury hears
item.description: A jury sees a covert police recording of Melbourne man Steve Fabriczy admitting to being involved in the burglary where Canberra grandmother Irma Palasics was killed in 1999, as he faces trial for her murder.

item.title: IVF embryo mix-up discovered 30 years later
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: Sri Lanka introduces four-day work week to conserve fuel amid Middle East war
item.description: Sri Lanka has introduced a shorter work week for people to use less fuel amid the ongoing Middle East war.

item.title: Israel claims it has killed Iran's security chief Ali Larijani. Who was he?
item.description: When Israeli and US strikes killed Ayatollah Ali Khamenei at the start of the Middle East war, Iran's security chief Ali Larijani became even more powerful than he had been for decades.

item.title: Women's Asian Cup 2026: Sam Kerr provides geometric genius as Katrina Gorry takes a battering — Matildas vs China quick hits
item.description: Sam Kerr gets her angles right, Katrina Gorry stands tall despite a battering and Kaitlyn Torpey has a birthday to remember. Here are the quick hits from the Matildas' exhilirating 2-1 semifinal triumph over China.

item.title: Historical IVF mix-up discovery raises questions about Australian industry
item.description: Fertility experts say the discovery of an IVF mix-up at Royal North Shore Hospital in 1995 could be the tip of the iceberg as more Australians research their parentage.

item.title: Travel quiz, March 18: Do you have the frequent flyers points to pass our test?
item.description: Which country’s currency is called the quetzal? And what is Thailand's Songkran festival best known for?

item.title: Behind Melbourne Museum's shiny facade sits a world hidden from view
item.description: Museums display only a fraction of their collection so we went behind the scenes at the Melbourne Museum to find the untold stories kept from public view.

item.title: Do Not Pass Go, a new dark comedy from Melbourne Theatre Company, might inspire you to quit your job
item.description: This dark comedy takes aim at capitalism and corporate work culture, but it also finds hope in the relationships between everyday people.

item.title: Middle East war forces pharmaceutical companies to reroute critical medicines to ensure Australian supply
item.description: The Middle East war is forcing major pharmaceutical companies to divert critical medicines away from one of the world's most important trade routes, to ensure there are no supply issues in Australia.

item.title: Reserve Bank governor warns of the recession we might have to have after interest rate hike
item.description: Is the RBA fighting last year's inflation war with its latest interest rate hike? Whatever the case may be, Australians are bracing for even more economic pain.

item.title: Labor hasn't got a capital gains tax policy yet, but it's already making the case
item.description: A Senate committee report into the capital gains tax was authored by the Greens but littered with clues about Labor's plans.

item.title: VIQ Solutions Australia placed into voluntary administration, court transcriptions services in limbo
item.description: Four weeks after the ABC revealed sensitive court files were being accessed offshore in a potential risk to national security, the company responsible for the breach has entered into voluntary administration.

item.title: Apartment height limits locked in across 25 Melbourne activity centres
item.description: The Victorian government has released its final planning controls for 25 activity centres in Melbourne, with tweaks meaning lower height limits in parts of Kew and Oakleigh and higher heights in parts of Coburg and Brunswick.

item.title: Court finds Japanese sterilisation law 'lacks rationality' and should be debated
item.description: A law in Japan bans childless women from accessing voluntary sterilisation. Now a Tokyo judge has found it "lacks rationality" and should be debated.

item.title: Cancer nurse banned for stealing medication from Royal Adelaide Hospital
item.description: A registered nurse has been banned for stealing opioid medication while working on an oncology ward at the Royal Adelaide Hospital, but a tribunal has allowed her to return to practise with strict conditions.

item.title: CSIRO survey finds drop in community support for Tasmanian salmon industry
item.description: A CSIRO report has found Tasmania's salmon farming industry has experienced a "significant and sustained shift" away from strong community support it experienced a decade ago.

item.title: In war-weary Lebanon, anger at Israel and Hezbollah takes hold
item.description: As Israel's intense bombardment continues into a third week, Lebanon's war-weary population and cash-strapped government are wondering when the fighting will stop.

item.title: Medical advocacy is crucial for young women but it can feel like a 'catch-22'
item.description: Young women have detailed their experiences being dismissed and misdiagnosed as chronic pain sufferers. Now, they're teaching others how to advocate for their health.

item.title: Women's Asian Cup 2026: Matildas' butterfly effect on display in win over China, but their evolution isn't complete
item.description: Sam Kerr has once again shown that she can single-handedly alter the course of Australian football history, but the rest of the team must follow after winning through to the Asian Cup final.

item.title: One Nation is tipped to be a major player in the South Australian election
item.description: One Nation South Australia candidate Rocco DeAngelis says people are "disappointed" in the major parties ahead of the state election, but Greens SA leader Robert Simms says the party is "scapegoating minority groups".

item.title: Multiple demolition derbies cancelled after SafeWork NSW rules barriers unsafe
item.description: Agriculture show societies say late, inconsistent rulings by SafeWork NSW about safety precautions for demolition derbies have cost them thousands in attendance and ticket sales.

item.title: Severe Cyclone Narelle upgraded to category three system
item.description: Tropical Cyclone Narelle could threaten Queensland coast as a category four system this week.

item.title: Scott Pendlebury avoids first ever suspension at AFL tribunal due to 'exceptional circumstances'
item.description: Collingwood great Scott Pendlebury avoids suspension despite being found guilty of rough conduct at the AFL tribunal.

item.title: Winter grain crop under pressure as fuel and fertiliser shortage hits at crunch time
item.description: Farmers are warning consumers to expect price rises in the supermarket as fuel and fertiliser shortages affect crop production and transport.

item.title: Kyle Sandilands says ARN Media has terminated his $100m radio contract
item.description: Kyle Sandilands has announced he has been sacked by ARN Media after an on-air row with co-host Jackie Henderson last month.

item.title: Tropical Cyclone Narelle on track to hit NT from Saturday
item.description: The Bureau of Meteorology is set to issue a cyclone watch alert for parts of the Northern Territory from tomorrow, as residents attempting to clean up after devastating floods brace for more rain.

item.title: Who is Anna Lapwood, the content creator bringing organ music to millions on social media
item.description: Anna Lapwood uses social media to share her love of the organ and favourite music from Bach to Lord of the Rings. Millions follow her and thousands more come to her concerts.

item.title: Matildas coach Joe Montemurro explains Hayley Raso absence in Asian Cup semifinal win over China
item.description: Matildas coach Joe Montemurro explains why star winger Hayley Raso was an unused substitute in the 2-1 semifinal win over China.

item.title: Police praise bravery of women who spoke out against Bunbury rapist masseur Anthony Brain
item.description: Detectives praise the bravery of more than a dozen women who testified against Bunbury massage therapist and rapist Anthony Brain, including Anwen Handmer.

item.title: Trump calls out Australia in blistering post declaring the US does not 'need' ally support in Iran
item.description: The United States president has declared America does "not need the help of anyone" in the war he launched against Iran, days after he called on allies to help reclaim a key shipping route.

item.title: Two men charged over fatal Bruce Highway crash in Burpengary
item.description: An allegedly stolen truck travelling the wrong way on the highway killed a man on his way home from work last month.

item.title: Police release new footage in appeal over grandfather Chris Baghsarian's murder
item.description: Police have released new video showing three men they believe were involved in the mistaken identity kidnapping and murder of Sydney grandfather Chris Baghsarian last month.

item.title: Senegal stripped of AFCON title, Morocco declared winner months after controversial final
item.description: Senegal's 1-0 win in the Africa Cup of Nations final has been overturned by an appeals board and a 3-0 victory awarded to Morocco after Senegalese players left the pitch in protest during the controversial January clash.

item.title: Forget fireworks, Bali's new year is marked with silence, fasting, meditation
item.description: Instead of fireworks and countdowns, Bali rings in its new year in complete silence.

item.title: Federal government suffers High Court loss over legality of curfews and ankle bracelets
item.description: New government measures to monitor people released from indefinite immigration detention are struck down by the High Court in Canberra.

item.title: Fuel supply chain coordinator to be proposed at national cabinet meeting
item.description: The prime minister will convene a virtual gathering of state and territory leaders tomorrow, with the talks to focus on strengthening state-federal cooperation in the face of demand-driven shortages on fuel.

item.title: Farmers and conservationists form 'emergency alliance' against invasive species
item.description: An unconventional union of environment and farming groups has been announced with a plan to take on a common enemy — invasive pests.

item.title: Denmark's King Frederik and Queen Mary spend second day in Melbourne
item.description: After a whirlwind of engagements yesterday, Danish King Frederik and Queen Mary open a renewable energy conference, meet with stem cell researchers and highlight youth mental health in Melbourne.

item.title: Silverton gardener's 'little oasis' featured in Gardening Australia magazine
item.description: At Helen Murray's home in Silverton in far west New South Wales, where temperatures often push past 30 degrees Celsius in summer, figuring out what will grow is often a jigsaw puzzle of trial and error.

item.title: John Travolta's old luxury jet bound for Australian aviation museum by ship
item.description: The long-awaited fuselage and wings of John Travolta's Boeing 707, which he is donating to a regional NSW aircraft museum, have been loaded onto a ship in the US, bound for Australia.

item.title: Police charge Qld Young Australian of the Year with possessing child exploitation material
item.description: Police allege illegal material was found on the youth support officer's work and personal devices. He has received several awards for his work with young people in the town of Cunnamulla after he founded a neighbourhood centre.

item.title: Man accused of woman and baby's murders suffers from schizoaffective disorder, court hears
item.description: Blake Seers has been charged with two counts of murder after the bodies of a 37-year-old woman and an 11-month-old girl were found inside a home at Belivah, south of Brisbane, last week.

item.title: Bank of America reaches tentative settlement over Epstein-linked transactions lawsuit
item.description: Bank of America has tentatively settled a lawsuit claiming it ignored suspicious financial transactions involving Jeffrey Epstein while he was sexually abusing hundreds of girls and women.

item.title: Aboriginal rangers meet on NSW South Coast to merge past and present
item.description: The first Aboriginal rangers conference for the NSW South Coast focused on how to best take care of country by combining cultural knowledge from elders with new technology.

item.title: Joe Kent joins growing list of Donald Trump allies distancing themselves from Iran war
item.description: After years of threats, insults and throwing the global world order into chaos, Donald Trump is being reminded at home and abroad that trust isn't just a nicety, it's essential when you need allies.

item.title: Fuel costs from Iran war forcing Australians to cancel domestic road travel, flights, cruises
item.description: Domestic travel is also taking a hit as a result of rising fuel costs across the globe, with regional communities likely to feel the most impact.

item.title: Is antibacterial or regular hand soap best for killing germs?
item.description: Experts say there is an array of options when it comes to hand soaps, and some labels can be misleading.

item.title: BHP names Brandon Craig CEO as Woodside confirms Elizabeth Westcott as leader
item.description: BHP has tapped Brandon Craig as its new CEO, while Elizabeth Westcott has taken the top job at Woodside Energy three months after she served as acting CEO.

item.title: Australian facilities damaged in Iranian attack on Middle East air base
item.description: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has revealed that an Iranian projectile hit a road just outside the Al Minhad Air Base, but no Australians were injured.

item.title: Body-in-suitcase double murderer Daniel Holdom appeals life sentences
item.description: Daniel Holdom is appealing his life sentences for dumping the body of a toddler in a suitcase after he killed her and her mother.

item.title: Tasmania to introduce planning law changes for 50 per cent larger granny flats
item.description: Tasmanians will soon be able to build second dwellings that are 50 per cent larger than the current size. An urban planner says other jurisdictions should follow suit.

item.title: Oil tankers have never stopped, industry assures, though they are in uncharted waters
item.description: Ships carrying oil are continuing to come to Australia as expected and should do so for the next month, and the industry is urging that while the picture beyond that is uncertain and complicated, "the tankers have never stopped coming".

item.title: Liberals defend SA election flyer that confused Palestinian flag with another
item.description: The South Australian Liberals have defended an election campaign flyer that attacks a Labor MP for his views on the Middle East and carries the slogan "Defend Australian Values", but appears to mistakenly show an image of a Sudanese flag instead of a Palestinian one.

item.title: Labour Hire Authority links worker exploitation in the Yarra Valley to organised crime
item.description: The Labour Hire Authority has accused an apple grower and labour-hire business of exploiting workers in the Yarra Valley, as concerns emerge about organised crime links.

item.title: Man faces court on murder charge after body found at Jung, Victoria
item.description: A Victorian man appeared agitated as he faced court on a murder charge after a body was found in the small town of Jung earlier this week.

item.title: NSW Police investigating DJ Haram for potential racial hate law breaches at Biennale of Sydney
item.description: In a video uploaded to social media, Zubeyda Muzeyyen, known as DJ Haram, can be heard urging attendees to "oppose the Zio-Australian-Epstein empire" before she led the crowd with a chant.

item.title: Cape York residents urged to make a plan to shelter from Cyclone Narelle
item.description: There are "places of refuge" but no designated cyclone shelters on Cape York Peninsula. Local leaders say residents need to make a plan to stay safe as a potential category four or five storm approaches the Queensland coast.

item.title: Bulldogs challenge Bronson Xerri to make case for NRL return, Dophins ready for Sharks fans' abuse
item.description: Cameron Ciraldo speaks for the first time since Bronson Xerri's discontent at the Bulldogs emerged over the weekend.

item.title: Cody Schaeffer writes to Governor-General to have Order of Australia revoked after guilty plea
item.description: Cody Schaeffer, who founded the charity Borderline Australia, last week pleaded guilty to assaulting five teenage boys he met through his work.

item.title: Trial date set in Bruce Lehrmann rape case in Toowoomba
item.description: Former Liberal staffer Bruce Lehrmann will stand trial on two counts of rape at a later date.

item.title: Man pleads not guilty to manslaughter of former WA Labor MP Barry Urban
item.description: A man has pleaded not guilty to the manslaughter of Barry Urban, the former Western Australian Labor politician who was allegedly attacked at a tyre business he managed in Perth.

item.title: Donald Trump, Xi Jinping and the meeting looming over Middle East war
item.description: The US president said last month that his planned visit to China on March 31 would be "a wild one". A lot has changed since that confident declaration and most of it has weakened his position.

item.title: Daly River evacuee's heartbreak at seeing community flooded for second time this year
item.description: Flood evacuees from Daly River in the remote Northern Territory — who have now spent more than a month this year in evacuation centres — say many had lost "everything they worked for".

item.title: Hawks warned not to underestimate Swans as AFL ladder leaders lose Isaac Heeney to injury
item.description: The Sydney Swans will be understrength after losing another key player to injury ahead of facing Hawthorn at the MCG tomorrow night.

item.title: Senior WA shire worker charged for fraud, corruption, stealing and perjury
item.description: A public officer in regional Western Australia has faced court on charges of attempted fraud, corruption and stealing while working for the Shire of Coorow.

item.title: Call for tougher anti-vilification penalties as hate flyers target Perth LGBTQIA+ community
item.description: Police are investigating flyers dropped in mailboxes across Perth targeting members of the LGBTQIA+ community.

item.title: Former Media Watch host Paul Barry weighs in on Kyle Sandilands sacking
item.description: Shock jock Kyle Sandilands was likely sacked because of pressure from the media regulator and his huge $100 million contract, former ABC Media Watch host Paul Barry says.

item.title: Michael James Chalmers faces court over death of 62-year-old woman in Footscray brothel in Melbourne's inner west
item.description: A forensic pathologist has told a committal hearing for Michael James Chalmers she believed the 62-year-old woman died from neck compression, smothering or gagging.

item.title: One of Australia's first steel mills in decades to be built in Ipswich, promising to support economy and Olympics
item.description: The developer claims its production technology can reduce carbon emissions by more than 70 per cent compared with the conventional steel-making process.

item.title: Possum found in Hobart Airport gift shop soft toy section
item.description: A real possum nestled among soft toys and animals in a gift shop has thrilled passengers — but caused "pandemonium" after going on a sightseeing tour of the departure lounge.

item.title: Pauline Hanson in confrontations with SA premier and MP as One Nation poll support continues
item.description: South Australia's premier tells Pauline Hanson he will work with any One Nation MPs elected on Saturday, as a new poll shows the right-wing party remains ahead of the Liberals.

item.title: Wednesday finance with Daniel Ziffer
item.description: News from the world of finance and business. Top stories in text, video and audio. Business video news bulletin. Features and analysis.

item.title: Japan storm into Asian Cup final against Matildas with ominous dismantling of South Korea
item.description: Japan has produced a frighteningly good display to overpower South Korea 4-1, booking a spot in the Women's Asian Cup final against the Matildas.

item.title: ACT Brumbies veteran James Slipper set to break Super Rugby's record for most games played
item.description: The prop will have played more Super Rugby than anyone else and is hoping to continue next season.

item.title: Why would Iranian players decline asylum?
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: What is a healthy birth weight? Urgency to close the gap for all babies
item.description: The Productivity Commission's latest Closing the Gap data shows just four out of 19 targets are on track. Advocates call for urgent funding to address low birth weights.

item.title: Jim Chalmers pushes ambitious budget as Middle East war fuels inflation
item.description: The treasurer will outline how the May federal budget will respond to the crisis with a focus on "substantial" savings, productivity and tax changes to make the system fairer for younger Australians.

item.title: Record property profits see home owner wealth surge despite rising interest rates
item.description: Australian home owners now have the protection of a "financial shield" in uncertain times, a report by property portal Domain has found, but record profits from property resales are widening the gap between established owners and aspiring first home buyers.

item.title: Australia to toughen tobacco crime laws as cigarette and vape black markets surge
item.description: Criminal gangs cashing in on Australia's illicit tobacco boom will face tougher penalties and expanded police surveillance powers in a fresh crackdown on the black market.

item.title: Military officers arrested over acid attack on human rights campaigner Andrie Yunus in Jakarta
item.description: Four Indonesian officers have been arrested and detained over an acid attack on human rights campaigner Andrie Yunus in Jakarta.

item.title: Iranian women's football team back in Iran, state media announces
item.description: The delegation of players and staff included five people who initially accepted Australia's offer of asylum but later changed their minds.

item.title: Treasury can't predict how Trump ends this war, with or without allies
item.description: Even the best minds at Treasury can't know what will happen with global oil supplies, or how Donald Trump plans to end this war, with or without the help of allies.

item.title: Heart surgeries in Tasmania reduced from daily to two a week amid indefinite industrial action
item.description: Daily heart surgeries have been cut to two a week in Tasmania while a small group of crucial specialists strikes for better pay and conditions. But negotiations with the government have halted, and locums are being sought to plug resulting healthcare gaps.

item.title: Ryan Gosling is humanity's last hope in the new space epic Project Hail Mary
item.description: Project Hail Mary hits Australian cinemas this week, joining Oscar-nominated animation Arco, off-the-wall slasher Dolly and BAFTA-winning biopic I Swear.

item.title: Put your Oscars knowledge to the test with our 2026 Academy Awards quiz
item.description: Awards season might be over for another year but the memories will live on in our minds … and in the questions of this Academy Awards quiz.

item.title: Steve Irwin made totemic crocodile Old Faithful famous. Recently laws may have been broken in its capture
item.description: Missing: One 80-year-old, 4.5 metre totemic crocodile. Last known whereabouts: Australia Zoo. Welcome to the fight for Old Faithful.

item.title: Why don't Australian banks offer 30-year fixed-rate home loans? Borrowers would benefit from them
item.description: A new report says Australian borrowers would benefit from 30-year fixed-rate home loans, and they should be made available.

item.title: Price gouging at petrol stations may not be illegal, experts warn, as PM considers supply chain tsar
item.description: Drivers fed up with massive price spikes at the bowser are being warned not to expect a wave of fuel company prosecutions over allegations of price gouging.

item.title: Could a fracture in conservative politics cost right-wing parties a seat in SA's parliament?
item.description: Labor's popularity in South Australia is set to lead to a large number of seats for the party at the upcoming state election, as various right-wing parties compete for a red seat in the upper house, writes ABC Chief Elections and Data Analyst Casey Briggs.

item.title: Some South Australians will vote in two elections this weekend
item.description: Thousands of First Nations South Australians will be eligible to vote in two elections on Saturday, in a potential historic first for the state.

item.title: Women's Asian Cup 2026: Matildas on notice as Japan soar into final — here's what we learnt from the second semifinal
item.description: Ultimate control, goals from everywhere and an eccentric coach from Greenland. This is what we learned from Japan's supremely impressive semifinal victory over South Korea.

item.title: Koala habitat logged by farmers caught unaware of new environment laws
item.description: Koala habitat has been bulldozed in an apparent breach of new environment laws that took effect in December, with fears farmers are unaware of their new obligations.

item.title: Checking online for fuel prices? Here's what to keep in mind
item.description: Petrol Spy, a national website that displays petrol, diesel and LPG prices across the country, says page views have gone up six to eight times since the Iran conflict started.

item.title: France bids to suspend Shein over childlike sex dolls
item.description: Shein is at the centre of a legal battle with France after investigations found childlike sex dolls were sold on its website.

item.title: Can ARN Media financially survive a legal battle with Kyle Sandilands and Jackie 'O' Henderson?
item.description: Even prior to this month's colossal blow-up of the Kyle and Jackie O Show, ARN Media's future had already been teetering on the edge.

item.title: Women share accounts of harassment and assault at informal kava groups
item.description: Women in Tongan communities speak out against sexual harassment at social events known as "kava gatherings", where men sit in a circle while a female server pours them the drink, known for its calming effects.

item.title: Live: PM urges end to war with Iran, as government prepares to release millions of litres of fuel
item.description: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese declares key objectives of the war against Iran have now been achieved, as he expresses hope the conflict will soon come to an end. Follow live.

item.title: Brisbane's 'third places' providing a spot away from work, home, and online echo chambers
item.description: In an increasingly digital world, Brisbane residents are still heading out to enjoy third places to take a break, enjoy life, and expose themselves to new ideas.

item.title: Cyclone Narelle tracker map: When will it hit the Queensland coast?
item.description: See the latest forecast and wind speeds as the system approaches Far North Queensland.

item.title: Squash meets volleyball — the 'secret' bush sport winning over new fans
item.description: Same game, different name: inside the quirky bush sport that takes volleyball onto a squash court.

item.title: The secret bush sport that's been hidden away for three decades
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Cost-of-living crisis widens regional-metro higher education gap
item.description: Pursuing higher education is difficult for regional students who uproot their lives, with advocates calling for support to level the playing field.

item.title: The 'catch-22' protecting Robodebt bureaucrats from criminal prosecution
item.description: Damning testimony will not land former Robodebt bureaucrats inside a courtroom, because the law empowering Australia's corruption watchdog to penetrate the right to silence is built with a shield.

item.title: Live: Wind gusts reach 315km/h as category five Cyclone Narelle nears Qld coast
item.description: Severe Tropical Cyclone Narelle has intensified into a category five system and is expected to cross the coast tomorrow morning. Follow live

item.title: Severe Tropical Cyclone Narelle to cross Qld coast as category five system
item.description: Severe Tropical Cyclone Narelle is expected to be a category five system when it crosses the coast, producing winds near its eye of 315 kilometres per hour.

item.title: PM says US, Israel's objectives in Iran have been 'secured' — as it happened
item.description: The prime minister holds a national cabinet meeting of premiers and chief ministers today to discuss fuel shortages in some communities.

item.title: ASX dips despite energy stock surge, Brent crude hits $US112 a barrel — as it happened
item.description: The ASX has been hit with a sell-off, while oil prices continue to gain.

item.title: Police investigate deaths of kangaroos and joey killed in Lake Macquarie hit-and-run
item.description: Police are investigating the deaths of kangaroos on suburban streets in Lake Macquarie in what has been described as a "deliberate" hit-and-run attack.

item.title: Senegal calls for corruption investigation after Africa Cup of Nations Nations title stripped
item.description: The Senegalese Football Federation plans to appeal the decision that gave its Africa Cup of Nations title to Morocco, with Senegal's government calling for an investigation into alleged corruption of African football's governing body.

item.title: Perth basketballer Roosevelt Williams Jr jailed for raping woman after night out
item.description: Semi-professional Perth basketballer Roosevelt Williams Jr is jailed for raping a friend following a night out, with the court told how he sent several text messages afterwards describing what he had done.

item.title: Danish royals finish Australia tour with day in Hobart
item.description: King Frederik of Denmark and Queen Mary have been welcomed to Hobart by a crowd happy to see the Danish Royals up close.

item.title: BOM declares cyclone watch for NT communities with Tropical Cyclone Narelle set to hit on Saturday
item.description: Parts of the Northern Territory have been issued a cyclone watch with Severe Tropical Cyclone Narelle expected to make landfall on Groote Eylandt from Saturday as a category three system.

item.title: Galatasaray player Noa Lang suffers freak hand injury, Barcelona knocks out Newcastle out of UEFA Champions League
item.description: Galatasaray forward Noa Lang suffered a freak hand injury when he caught his thumb on an advertising hoarding in his team's 4-0 loss to Liverpool in the Champions League.

item.title: Why did the RBA raise interest rates? Will higher petrol prices have same effect?
item.description: This week's rate rise left ABC readers asking "how would hiking rates stop inflation caused by an unavoidable cost like petrol that's spiking due to a war on the other side of the world?"

item.title: Sydney's inner west has the conditions for a cycling boom, but riders say the network falls short
item.description: Critics say more separated bike lanes are needed to close gaps and make riding safe for a wider range of people.

item.title: AFL news wrap: Matt Rowell set to see physio on broken finger, Hawks prepare to reach 'top notch' against Swans
item.description: Gold Coast has started its 2026 campaign well, even while last year's Brownlow medallist continues to be sidelined due to a broken finger.

item.title: Man charged with arson and stealing after fire destroys PCYC building
item.description: A 28-year-old man has been charged with stealing and arson after the Toowoomba PCYC was destroyed by fire, as the organisation says replacing it could cost up to $20 million.

item.title: Dance performance takes on expectations of disability, demanding the right to take risks
item.description: Challenging assumptions about what disabled people can and can't do is at the centre of this performance at the Joondalup Festival.

item.title: Victorian crime statistics show youth offenders over-represented in carjackings, home invasions
item.description: The latest figures from Victoria's Crime Statistics Agency show minors committed more than half of all carjackings and home invasions last year.

item.title: NRL news: Dragons forward Loko Pasifiki Tonga cleared of serious injury after being hospitalised
item.description: A Dragons training session ends with a player stretchered off and assisted from the ground into an ambulance before being rushed to hospital.

item.title: Cable Beach venue in Western Australia's far north charged with liquor breaches
item.description: A cafe in the Kimberley town that has strict alcohol restrictions has been charged with multiple liquor breaches, while two of its workers have been fined.

item.title: Australia isn't sending a warship to the Persian Gulf. But if the government wanted to, is there one to send?
item.description: Australia has made it clear it's not planning to send a warship to the Strait of Hormuz and naval experts warn our ships would be of minimal help if they were deployed.

item.title: SA Victims' Rights Commissioner describes Rohan Dennis's post as 'deeply offensive'
item.description: A post shared on Instagram by former professional cyclist Rohan Dennis, whose wife died after being struck by a car he was driving, has been described as "deeply offensive".

item.title: Ship crew involved in French Polynesia cocaine bust sent to immigration detention
item.description: The crew of a vessel that was intercepted carrying nearly 5 tonnes of cocaine has been escorted off the ship by Australian authorities.

item.title: Iranian oil tankers are using fake data to get sanctioned fuel out of the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Changing flags, creating new names, and faking their GPS data, ABC NEWS Verify investigates how Iranian-linked oil tankers continue to export oil during the war.

item.title: Who in the Iranian leadership has been killed?
item.description: A growing list of Iran's top leadership has been reportedly killed in ongoing strikes by Israel and the United States.

item.title: Byron Bay police officer, a former Bondi Rescue lifeguard, makes dramatic surf rescue
item.description: A former Bondi Rescue reality TV lifeguard who now works as a police officer in Byron Bay jumped into the surf to rescue three French tourists off a rocky headland.

item.title: Botanist gave early warning on rehab difficulties for troubled Griffin Coal's Collie mine site
item.description: The WA government insists Griffin Coal's obligations have not changed, but changes to its mining agreement could allow the company to walk away from rehabilitating its Collie mine site.

item.title: Albanese unveils new national fuel supply taskforce to steer response to Middle East war
item.description: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has named Anthea Harris, a former chief of Australia's energy regulator, as the national coordinator of the taskforce.

item.title: Legendary electronica group Faithless' Sister Bliss reveals how their explosive debut album came to be
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: '16 years in the making': Renowned Let Them Theory author Mel Robbins on her success
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Prolonged Middle East war and AI profitability 'downgrades' are major risks for economy, RBA says
item.description: Interest rates, war in the Middle East and AI advancements are expected to hit the Australian economy, but the RBA says we are prepared.

item.title: Israel hits Iran South Pars facilities in world's largest natural gas field
item.description: The Israeli bombing of Iranian facilities in the South Pars gas field and Iran's retaliation targeting energy infrastructure across the Persian Gulf, mark a significant point of escalation in the US-Israeli war with Iran.

item.title: Driver was on phone when she killed young motorcyclist, judge says
item.description: A driver who has avoided jail after she fatally struck a 21-year-old motorcyclist was under the influence of cannabis and on the phone at the time of the crash south of Adelaide, a judge says.

item.title: Unemployment rate rises to 4.3 per cent, but rate hike fears persist amid inflation concerns
item.description: Australia's unemployment rate rose to 4.3 per cent in February, up from 4.1 per cent, in seasonally adjusted terms.

item.title: Bundaberg residents call for buyback scheme after three major floods in 16 years
item.description: There are calls for the Queensland government to buy back homes in Bundaberg's flood zones, but the disaster recovery minister says the focus is on mitigation measures like floodgates.

item.title: Iran war leaves Japan and South Korea on edge, as US moves assets to Middle East
item.description: As the Iran war stretches on there are mounting concerns in the Asia-Pacific that a protracted conflict, and a distracted White House, could threaten peace in the region.

item.title: Iran will not boycott FIFA World Cup, but seeks to get United States-based matches moved
item.description: Iran has no intention of boycotting the FIFA World Cup, but is seeking to have its group stage matches moved out of the United States.

item.title: Husband pleads not guilty to careless driving charge over wife's death
item.description: Haydn Thomas Mansell pleads not guilty to careless driving causing death after the vehicle he was driving collided with a freight train at a level crossing near Albany.

item.title: What Doesn't Kill You | Ghanim Al Shnen
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Victorian Premier Jacinta Allan dismisses leadership rumblings as 'scallywag' gossip
item.description: The Victorian premier denounces speculation about her leadership as "scallywag" gossip amid growing concern that she could cost Labor government in November's state election.

item.title: French jockey Marina Morel's death subject of inquest
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Coroner probes French jockey Marina Morel's death at Gulgong
item.description: Marina Morel came to Australia from France to follow her dream of being a professional jockey, but died after the horse she was riding collapsed.

item.title: Steph Catley says Asian Cup final not 'mission impossible' for Matildas despite ominous Japan form
item.description: The Matildas aren't daunted by Japan's ominous form ahead of what shapes up as a blockbuster Asian Cup final.

item.title: Kangaroo Island considers introducing 'last cat policy' in bid to eradicate feral cats
item.description: Kangaroo Island Council is considering banning the introduction of all cats to the island, in a move the mayor is calling the "last cat policy". If successful, the ban would make the South Australian island one of the world's biggest inhabited islands to be free of cats.

item.title: NSW man sentenced to eight years' jail for role in $15m drug operation
item.description: Nathan John Bailey will be eligible for parole next year, after pleading guilty to importing a controlled substance.

item.title: Family who conceived through IVF at Royal North Shore Hospital seeks DNA testing
item.description: A family is pursuing DNA testing after hearing about an IVF mix-up at the same Sydney fertility clinic used to conceive its baby.

item.title: How boosting vaccinations could prevent meningitis outbreak in Australia
item.description: A growing outbreak of meningococcal B and meningitis has killed two young people in the UK as the number of infections climb. Here's why infectious disease professor Paul Griffin thinks Australia should be worried.

item.title: Inside Lizzy Hoo's wild life escapades: music, Mongolia and the moments that shaped her
item.description: Comedian Lizzy Hoo loves an adventure, which is how she ended up at a music festival outside Ulaanbaatar watching her new favourite band.

item.title: Albanese hopeful about end of war against Iran as budget looms
item.description: Perhaps the prime minister was hoping US President Donald Trump was tuning in to Triple M when he claimed that the attack on Iran had now achieved its aims, and offered an off-ramp for the war to end.

item.title: Taskforce to help communities hit by Great Western Highway closure
item.description: Locals, industry groups promised input into a support package for communities struggling to cope with the ongoing closure of the Great Western Highway.

item.title: US actor Kevin Spacey avoids new trial with court settlement
item.description: Hollywood actor Kevin Spacey avoids a new trial after settling out of court with three men who launched a UK civil suit against him accusing him of sexual assault.

item.title: Teenage boy charged after woman stabbed at Clayton shopping centre in Melbourne's south east
item.description: The 25-year-old woman is in a serious condition in hospital after being stabbed inside a Clayton shopping centre.

item.title: Power prices set to fall, but Iran war casts long shadow over bill relief
item.description: Benchmark power prices across parts of the country will ease as renewables reduce wholesale costs for electricity, but the regulator warns its modelling does not account for the war in the Middle East.

item.title: Health insurance premiums to hike as much as 25 per cent, CHOICE analysis shows
item.description: Some Australians with private health insurance will face premium increases of as much as 25 per cent from next month, according to new analysis from CHOICE.

item.title: BOM's satellite feed showing the approach of Tropical Cyclone Narelle
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Residents dismayed by National Capital Authority's proposed new high-density Canberra precinct
item.description: The National Capital Authority wants to define a new urban hub in the nation's capital, but its proposed new building height limits are creating angst.

item.title: SA Liberals and Labor differ wildly on how much they say election promises will cost
item.description: Labor says the opposition's promises will cost $2.4 billion more than they say, as both parties target the public sector as a way to save money on the budget ahead of the state election.

item.title: Artist Ben Quilty makes case for culture in a room focused on roads
item.description: One of Australia's most acclaimed artists brought humour and frustration to a council meeting weighing commerce against culture.

item.title: Danish Royals meet and greet in Hobart
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Industry experts speculate on podcasts, radio prospects for Kyle Sandilands and Jackie O
item.description: The collapse of The Kyle and Jackie O Show has left hundreds of thousands of people looking for something else to listen to in the morning, with some industry insiders questioning whether the duo could make a comeback.

item.title: Notorious outback murderer Bradley Murdoch refused to watch video from Peter Falconio's parents, inquest hears
item.description: Bradley Murdoch took the location of his victim Peter Falconio's remains to his grave. A coronial inquest has revealed police made two desperate attempts to get answers just days before he died.

item.title: Tony Popovic says young players have 'great opportunity' with Socceroos despite Adrian Segečić's defection to Croatia
item.description: Socceroos coach Tony Popovic maintains young players have an opportunity to represent Australia at the highest level despite losing a younger player to a European rival.

item.title: Residents also have concerns about the 'self-evacuations' from Jilkminggan
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Remote residents concerned about withheld flood relief payments as Cyclone Narelle looms
item.description: Stranded residents of Jilkminggan in the Big Rivers region say they need their full flood relief payments now, after finding out this week they would only get one-quarter of the money while they remain in shelters in Mataranka.

item.title: Hong Kong review panel hears all safety measures failed on day of fatal Wang Fuk Court fire
item.description: Evidence during the first hearing into the cause of a Hong Kong apartment complex blaze has heard a series of failures contributed to the deaths of 168 people.

item.title: Country footballers jailed for sexually abusing teenage girl at a party on Victoria's Surf Coast
item.description: A judge described Jaiden Fidge and Riley Smith's offending as having an element of "abhorrent male bravado", with the court hearing one of the footballers boasted about the assault.

item.title: Live: Firerce contest at MCG as scoring dries up
item.description: A Sydney side missing Errol Gulden and Isaac Heeney heads to the MCG to take on Hawthorn in a bid to keep their 100 per cent record alive. Follow live.

item.title: WA Police Homicide Squad investigating after woman found dead at Banksia Grove home
item.description: WA Police investigate the death of a woman at a home in Perth's northern suburbs, with a man aged in his 40s assisting detectives with their inquiries.

item.title: Thursday Finance with David Chau
item.description: News from the world of finance and business. Top stories in text, video and audio. Business video news bulletin. Features and analysis.

item.title: Live: Bulldogs hold narrow lead over Raiders in torrential conditions
item.description: Canterbury are just in front after a wet and wild first half against an ill-disciplined Canberra side. Follow live.

item.title: Report reveals major gaps in WA's response to family and domestic violence
item.description: A report tabled in parliament finds significant gaps in how agencies respond to high-risk family and domestic violence, as a prominent advocate calls for urgent action.

item.title: Sunshine Coast childcare worker Arvind Ajay Singh guilty of indecent treatment of child
item.description: A former childcare worker has been found not guilty of raping a child in his care after a three-day trial on the Sunshine Coast.
